everyone.name: Lori Lorusso
everyone.bio: Lori has a passion and enthusiasm for working with the developer and open source community. She is a CNCF Ambassador, former CNCF Marketing Committee Chair, former Chair of the CDF Outreach Marketing Committee, program chair of cdCon 2023, and is active in the OpenSSF devrel committee. She co-hosts the CD Pipeline on behalf of the CDF with TechstrongTV. She is committed to helping open source and other tech communities grow and adapt in our ever changing environment. She is currently Head of Community for Percona.

everyone.name: Bmk Lakshminarayanan
everyone.bio: Transformation Architect, SECTION6 BMK is an inspiring and passionate DevOps Advocate and Value Stream Architect promoting DevOps, Lean and Value Stream Management principles & practices. He is also a New Zealand ambassador for the Cloud-Native Computing Foundation(CNCF), DevOps Institute and a Board Advisor for the Value Stream Management consortium(VSMC). He hosts the CNCF meetup in Wellington, New Zealand and is co-chair of Cloud-Native Summit A/NZ. He blogs about his experiences and learnings at https://bmk.nz/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: 1. Promoting Open Source and Cloud-Native projects and ecosystem tools in our community. 2. Connecting with experts, maintainers, contributors and other Opensource and cloud-native thought leaders to amplify my learning 3. Use the ambassador program and help in bringing in the best to my community in terms of cloud-native learnings, education and training.

everyone.name: Giorgi Keratishvili
everyone.bio: Giorgi is a seasoned IT professional with over a decade of experience spanning both Development and Operations. His expertise covers a wide range from baremetal infrastructure to advanced automation of cloud, Agile transformations, container orchestration, Platform Engineering, SRE, Incident Management, and Cloud Native maturity. Beyond his professional work, Giorgi is a passionate community leader. He serves as an AWS Community Builder & User Group Leader in Tbilisi, CNCF Chapter Lead and Golden Kubestronaut, CDF Ambassador, IEEE Senior Member, and Ambassador for the Institute of DevOps. He actively organizes meetups and workshops to foster awareness and growth in DevOps, SRE and Platform Engineering across the Georgian tech community and beyond.All about kubernetes, cloud and AI native

everyone.name: Amruta Shetye
everyone.bio: I have spent over nine years as a DevOps Consultant and Trainer, helping global organizations build better software. My expertise lies in the Kubernetes ecosystem, where I love turning complex infrastructure concepts into practical, real-world solutions. I am deeply passionate about cloud-native technologies and enjoy sharing that excitement through teaching. For me, it isn’t just about the tools; it’s about empowering engineers to truly understand and own their environments.

everyone.name: Mohamed Chaaben
everyone.bio: I'm DevOps Engineer at AWS. I build and scale cloud infrastructure using AWS and Kubernetes. My work focuses on implementing cloud-native technologies that deliver reliable, high-performance solutions for our customers.

everyone.name: Issara Wattana
everyone.bio: Technology Architect focused on building scalable Kubernetes platforms and CI/CD-driven delivery. I specialize in applying CNCF technologies to design sustainable, real-world systems that evolve with the business.

ambassadors.name: Lori Lorusso
ambassadors.bio: Lori has a passion and enthusiasm for working with the developer and open source community. She is a CNCF Ambassador, former CNCF Marketing Committee Chair, former Chair of the CDF Outreach Marketing Committee, program chair of cdCon 2023, and is active in the OpenSSF devrel committee. She co-hosts the CD Pipeline on behalf of the CDF with TechstrongTV. She is committed to helping open source and other tech communities grow and adapt in our ever changing environment. She is currently Head of Community for Percona.

ambassadors.name: Nicola Marco Decandia
ambassadors.bio: I am a seasoned cloud-native professional with over 20 years of experience in the industry, specializing in Kubernetes, containerization, and DevOps practices. As the founder and organizer of the Bari and Matera CNCF Cloud-Native Meetup, I have fostered a strong local community committed to sharing knowledge and best practices. My technical expertise spans across multiple CNCF projects, including Prometheus, Envoy, and Helm. I regularly share my insights and experiences on my blog at www.tanzu.eu, where I explore emerging trends and innovative solutions in the cloud-native ecosystem. Passionate about continuous learning and collaboration, I am excited to contribute to the community as a CNCF Ambassador.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am thrilled to be an Ambassador because it allows me to actively contribute to the growth and development of the cloud-native ecosystem, which I am truly passionate about. This role provides a unique platform to engage with a diverse and talented community, enabling the exchange of knowledge and ideas that drive innovation. Furthermore, being an Ambassador presents the opportunity to mentor and inspire others while learning from the collective experiences of fellow professionals. It is an honor to serve as a bridge between the CNCF and the broader community, fostering collaboration and mutual support.

ambassadors.name: Viktor Farcic
ambassadors.bio: Viktor Farcic is a lead rapscallion at Upbound and a published author. He is a host of the YouTube channel DevOps Toolkit and a co-host of DevOps Paradox.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Viktor Farcic is excited to join the CNCF Ambassadors program because he believes in the power of open source software to transform the world. He is passionate about helping others learn about and adopt CNCF technologies, and he is eager to contribute to the CNCF community in new and innovative ways. Viktor is also excited to be part of a team of dedicated and talented individuals who are working to make the world a better place through open source software.

ambassadors.name: Sara Trappetti
ambassadors.bio: I started my journey in Tech Marketing in 2016, and in 2021, I embarked with SIGHUP on the Cloud Native industry as a complete newbie. For the first six months, I felt overwhelmed by the vastness and complexity of the field, but then something amazing happened along the way – thanks to my stubbornness and the patient support of my colleagues, I actively started contributing to the Cloud Native community and found a perfect place to express my skills while helping others, organizing events, and supporting projects - I'm a KCD Italy and Cloud Native Milano Meetup organizer and part of the Glossary Italian Localization Group. All that apart, I'm music-passionate and a restless shower performer, a winter couch knitter and cat cuddler, a balcony gardening ace, a dabble baker, and a great backwarmer during adventurous motorcycle journeys.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a non-tech member and a proud CNCF ambassador, I'm determined to lend my support in any way possible. I'm here to share the lessons I've learned, contribute to the growth of our community, and empower others to discover the Cloud Native Community and find their own path. I want to be a guiding hand to help others navigate CNCF initiatives and projects. It's all about showing that there are different ways to contribute and various subgroups tailored to their expertise.

ambassadors.name: Kevin Evans
ambassadors.bio: Kevin Evans is a Senior Technical Specialist at Microsoft, focusing on Azure solutions with over 25 years of experience in the technology sector. He is the founder of the Code to Cloud community, dedicated to advancing cloud-native practices in Western Canada. As a recognized thought leader, Kevin also serves as the chapter lead for the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in Calgary, Alberta. Where he actively supports the cloud community. Through his expertise in architecture and technical engineering, he is committed to empowering businesses with secure, scalable cloud solutions. Kevin also hosts the Code to Cloud podcast, sharing insights and fostering knowledge sharing across the cloud technology space. Website: codetocloud.io

ambassadors.name: Michael Levan
ambassadors.bio: Michael Levan is a seasoned engineer and consultant in the Kubernetes and Platform Engineering space who spends his time working with startups and enterprises around the globe on consulting, training, and content creation. He is a trainer, 3x published author, podcast host, international public speaker, and was part of the Kubernetes v1.28 Release Team.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The avenue that's now opened to work with more people around the world. I've been giving back to the community, creating free content, and contributing to projects for 5+ years. I'm now excited to work with others who are passionate about doing the same.

ambassadors.name: 子篆 林
ambassadors.bio: My current focus is cloud-native computing, programming languages, and system software. I'm working as a compiler developer at secondstate. My blog https://dannypsnl.me writes technical content about formal methods and mathematics. I'm an open source technology enthusiast, and very excited to push formal methods in cloud computing.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm passionate about helping people as I could, been an ambassador is a well way to do so. Besides that, this is a good opportunities do learn from others, like how to maintain communities, or their invaluable personal perspectives.

ambassadors.name: Márk Sági-Kazár
ambassadors.bio: Márk is a SWE/SRE from Hungary with years of experience in various Cloud Native technologies as well as contributing to and maintaining Open Source Software. He is one of the core maintainers of Dex, a Cloud Native identity federation solution and a number of Go libraries (Viper being the most prominent among them). Márk also organizes several tech meetups in Hungary (Go Budapest, Kubernetes and Cloud Native Budapest, Microservices Budapest) and regularly appears as a speaker in the region at meetups, workshops, conferences and podcasts. You can occasionally find him posting to his blog (https://sagikazarmark.hu/), but chances are his GitHub profile is more interesting. In his free time, Márk likes riding a bicycle, reading books, but his favorite hobby for more than 20 years has been Hungarian folk dance.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I always liked learning/building new technologies and then sharing the knowledge with others. By working on various Open Source projects for more than a decade I also learned to appreciate the opportunity to learn from people coming from different backgrounds. By being an ambassador I hope that I'll have more opportunities to connect with people, learn from them and maybe share some of my knowledge as well.

kubestronauts.name: Amruta Shetye
kubestronauts.bio: I have spent over nine years as a DevOps Consultant and Trainer, helping global organizations build better software. My expertise lies in the Kubernetes ecosystem, where I love turning complex infrastructure concepts into practical, real-world solutions. I am deeply passionate about cloud-native technologies and enjoy sharing that excitement through teaching. For me, it isn’t just about the tools; it’s about empowering engineers to truly understand and own their environments.

kubestronauts.name: Peter Stolz
kubestronauts.bio: I studied CyberSecurity and work in Security and DevOps. I use a lot of Falco, argo and NATS. I like playing CTFs and learning about cool projects to host in my homelab.

kubestronauts.name: Rajeev Ranjan Sinha
kubestronauts.bio: My name is Rajeev Sinha and technology excites me. I have close to 18 years of IT experience in various domains - Linux/Unix/Clusters/ Storage /Backup/ Kubernetes/ CI-CD / Monitoring - splunk/ Prometheus/grafana…etc. CNCF technologies- kubernetes / Prometheus

kubestronauts.name: Jialiang Gao
kubestronauts.bio: I am a hands-on Cloud & Data/AI Architect in building and delivering complex, scalable cloud-native platforms. I am specialized in Google Cloud, Kubernetes, DevOps, Data Platform and advanced AI technologies. My focus is on architectural design, implementation, and fostering innovation.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Lavanya Anbalagan
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am an enthusiastic learner with over five years of experience in enterprise container platforms and holds a Kubestronaut title. In addition to my professional achievements, I have a personal interest in exploring new places and maintaining physical fitness.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Chandra Vemu
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Chandra is a technical consultant working across Kubernetes, OpenShift, and Ansible Automation to help enterprises design and scale hybrid cloud platforms. He collaborates with banking and telco clients across the Middle East and Asia to drive automation and modernization initiatives.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Christopher Adigun
goldenKubestronauts.bio: As a Cloud Native Telco Architect, I specialize in designing telecommunications networks using Kubernetes and related software.My expertise includes advanced Kubernetes networking solutions like Multus, Whereabouts, SRIOV-Device-Plugin, Spider-Pool, and Userplane Acceleration (DPDK).I'm passionate about sharing my knowledge and have created a Udemy course on Kubernetes networking (https://www.udemy.com/course/kubernetes-baremetal-networking-using-gns3).Additionally, I create content about Kubernetes-related topics on my Medium blog (https://medium.com/@futuredon) and YouTube (https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCuAdwhfPswT1hDezzRGnTKg)

goldenKubestronauts.name: Christophe Calvet
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Christophe Calvet is a technical architect specialized in virtualization technologies, currently expanding into Kubernetes and cloud-native solutions.

maintainers.name: Michael McCune
maintainers.bio: lover of open source and late night coding
